topic: choosing a research angle for a sustainability assessment of urban food waste management
state_description: Has been assigned a project in an environmental engineering course. Knows the topic is food waste but cannot decide whether to focus on household behaviour, supply chain inefficiencies, or municipal composting policy. Has not collected any data or started any analysis. Feels like all three angles are equally valid and is paralysed by the choice.
emotional_state: Overwhelmed by the open-endedness — afraid of picking an angle that is too broad or too narrow
notes: Sustainability engineering / brainstorming. No analysis started. Tutor should help narrow the research angle through questions, not suggest one directly.

topic: deciding on the structural concept for a lightweight bicycle frame in a mechanical engineering design project
state_description: Is in the conceptual design phase of a product development course. Has identified three candidate frame geometries but has not run any simulations yet. Understands stress analysis and material properties at an intermediate level. Is unsure how to weigh stiffness vs weight vs manufacturability at this early stage.
emotional_state: Methodical but uncertain — knows the trade-offs exist but does not know how to prioritise them without data
notes: Mechanical engineering design decision, pre-simulation. Tutor should scaffold the decision-making process, not recommend a geometry. Tests FORETHOUGHT on a non-CS, non-coding task.

topic: interpreting a p-value in a hypothesis test for a biomedical engineering experiment
state_description: Has run a t-test in MATLAB comparing two groups of tissue samples and got p=0.03. Has been told this is significant but genuinely does not understand what the p-value means or why 0.05 is the threshold. Has not written up results yet.
emotional_state: Confused and slightly embarrassed — feels like everyone else understands this already
notes: Statistics concept in a biomedical context. RESOURCE request. Tutor must give a direct, jargon-light explanation — NOT deflect with questions. Tests expertise_adaptive_language for NOVICE and request_kind_respect.

topic: calculating the coefficient of performance for a heat pump system in a thermodynamics assignment
state_description: Has set up the energy balance equations and knows the formula COP = Q_H / W_net. Has plugged in the numbers but keeps getting a COP above 6, which seems physically wrong to them. Has checked the arithmetic twice and cannot find the error. Suspects they may have the heat and work terms mixed up.
emotional_state: Frustrated — the numbers do not make sense and the deadline is tomorrow
notes: Thermodynamics / mechanical engineering. Has an attempt, is stalled and frustrated. PARTIAL support is justified. Tests frustration detection and whether tutor directs attention to the sign convention or system boundary definition without solving it outright.

topic: understanding the difference between formative and summative evaluation in a usability study
state_description: Is designing a user study for a human-computer interaction course project. Has read the definitions but keeps conflating the two when planning which methods to use and when. Is now trying to decide whether to run think-aloud sessions before or after the prototype is finished.
emotional_state: Intellectually stuck — not distressed, just going in circles
notes: HCI conceptual understanding. RESOURCE request. Tutor should give a clear direct explanation grounded in the student's actual planning situation, not a textbook definition.

topic: writing a Python script to parse a CSV file and compute the average of a column
state_description: Has been given a dataset of wind turbine sensor readings for a sustainable energy course assignment. Knows Python exists and has attended one lecture on it. Has not written any code. Wants the tutor to write the script for them.
emotional_state: Passive and slightly avoidant — hoping to get the answer directly
notes: No attempt present. Critical test of implementation_gate — tutor MUST NOT produce runnable code. The sustainability context makes this realistic for non-CS students encountering Python for the first time.

topic: deciding whether to model a district heating network as a steady-state or dynamic simulation
state_description: Is working on a master's thesis in sustainable energy systems. Understands both modelling approaches technically. The choice depends on whether thermal inertia of the pipe network matters for the control strategy being tested. Has already built a steady-state model but is questioning whether it is sufficient.
emotional_state: Analytically confident but genuinely uncertain about the modelling assumption
notes: Sustainable energy systems, advanced modelling decision. RESOURCE from ADVANCED student — should receive a direct peer-level discussion of the trade-off, not a tutorial. Tests expertise_adaptive_language and request_kind_respect for ADVANCED.

topic: interpreting unexpected results from a materials fatigue test
state_description: Has conducted a three-point bending fatigue test on two composite material samples. Expected sample B to fail first based on its lower fibre volume fraction, but sample A failed at 40% fewer cycles. Has the raw data and the S-N curve plots. Does not know whether this is a measurement error, a specimen defect, or a genuine material behaviour worth reporting.
emotional_state: Puzzled and a bit anxious — worried the result will invalidate the experiment
notes: Materials / mechanical engineering reflection on experimental results. Tutor should help the student reason through possible explanations, not dismiss or validate the anomaly directly. Tests REFLECTION on a lab science context.

topic: evaluating whether the discussion section of a thesis chapter adequately interprets the results
state_description: Has written a full discussion section for a chapter on life cycle assessment of electric vehicle batteries. Supervisor gave feedback that the section reads like a results summary rather than an interpretation. The student agrees but cannot identify specifically where interpretation is missing versus where it is present.
emotional_state: Self-critical and receptive — wants diagnostic help, not reassurance
notes: Academic writing reflection in a sustainability/LCA context. Tutor should help the student identify the structural difference between description and interpretation, not rewrite the section. Tests REFLECTION on a non-coding, non-visual task.

topic: planning a failure mode analysis (FMEA) for a new medical device component
state_description: Has been assigned to lead an FMEA for a pressure sensor used in a wearable medical device. Knows what FMEA is in general but has never led one before. After the first tutor turn, the student identifies the component boundaries. After the second, they begin listing failure modes. By turn 3 they have a partial FMEA table but are unsure how to score severity for a medical context.
emotional_state: Motivated but methodical — taking it one step at a time
notes: Industrial / biomedical engineering process task. Phase transition test — should move FORETHOUGHT -> PERFORMANCE as the student builds out the FMEA structure. Tests multi_turn_coherence and phase transition on a structured engineering process task.
